meaning: I'm in (such a) mood / I'm in the zone
communicative_purpose: To indicate being in a particular mood or state
trigger_situations: Describing current mood, setting expectations, explaining behavior
contexts: Social media, morning messages, explaining reactions, personality expressions
variations: je suis en mode, je suis en mode paresse, je suis en mode productive
common_mistakes: Overusing without specifying mode; mixing casual and formal moods
example_1: Je suis en mode zéro motivation ce matin, laisse-moi tranquille.
example_2: En mode chill ce week-end, pas de plans, juste du repos.
example_3: Je suis en mode marathon series, j'ai 5 saisons à binge watcher.
example_1_translation: I'm in zero motivation mode this morning, leave me alone.
example_2_translation: In chill mode this weekend, no plans, just rest.
example_3_translation: I'm in marathon series mode, I have 5 seasons to binge watch.

communicative_purpose: To say a place has no atmosphere or nothing is happening
trigger_situations: Empty venues, boring events, no activity, lifeless situations
contexts: Nightlife, social events, social media, group chats
variations: c'est dead, c'est dead total, c'est trop dead, y'a personne
common_mistakes: Literal death interpretation; mixing with English meaning of dead
example_1: Ce bar est dead un mardi, personne ne sort le mardi.
example_2: Insta story sans réponses, c'est dead quand personne regarde.
example_3: Cette conversation de groupe est dead depuis des semaines.
example_1_translation: This bar is dead on a Tuesday, no one goes out on Tuesday.
example_2_translation: Insta story without replies, it's dead when no one is watching.
example_3_translation: This group chat has been dead for weeks.

communicative_purpose: To express being very impressed or overwhelmed by something
trigger_situations: After amazing performances, shocking news, incredible experiences
contexts: Concerts, art, movies, surprises, overwhelming emotions
variations: je suis blowé, je suis blowée, je suis impressionné, je suis scotché
example_1: Ce concert était insane, je suis blowé !
example_2: Le talent de cette artiste, je suis blowée personnellement.
example_3: On a eu la surprise, je suis blowé par la performance.
example_1_translation: This concert was insane, I'm blown away!
example_2_translation: I am personally blown away by the talent of this artist.
example_3_translation: We were surprised, I was blown away by the performance.

meaning: what do you like / what are you into
communicative_purpose: To ask about someone's interests or preferences
trigger_situations: Getting to know someone, bonding, finding common ground
contexts: Dating apps, new friendships, ice breakers, casual conversations
variations: tu kiffes quoi, t'es fan de quoi, t'aime quoi, tes centers d'intérêt
common_mistakes: Too direct for some cultures; using 'kiffer' in formal contexts
example_1: Salut ! Tu kiffes quoi dans la vie ? On a peut-être des affinités.
example_2: Tu kiffes quel genre musical ? Je cherche des recommendations.
example_3: T'es fan de quoi comme series ? J'ai besoin de binge-watch ideas.
example_1_translation: Hi ! What do you like in life? Maybe we have affinities.
example_2_translation: What genre of music do you like? I'm looking for recommendations.
example_3_translation: What series are you a fan of? I need binge-watch ideas.

communicative_purpose: To express that something is bothersome, tedious, or annoying
trigger_situations: Dealing with tedious tasks, annoying situations, boring things
contexts: Work complaints, tedious errands, boring events, technical issues
variations: c'est relou, c'est reloube, c'est ennuyeux, c'est chiant, c'est emmerdant (vulgar)
collocates: cette situation, ce projet, ce traffic, cette attente
example_1: Le traffic pour aller bosser, c'est relou tous les matins.
example_3: Les réveils à 5h pour attraper le train, c'est vraiment relou.
example_1_translation: The traffic to go to work is annoying every morning.
example_2_translation: This administrative paperwork is too annoying.
example_3_translation: Waking up at 5 a.m. to catch the train is really annoying.

meaning: I was thinking that / I thought to myself
communicative_purpose: To introduce a thought, idea, or reflection
trigger_situations: Sharing thoughts, making suggestions, reflecting on situations
contexts: Conversations, brainstorming, friendly advice, philosophical moments
variations: je me disais que, je me disais, je me suis dit que, comme je me disais
common_mistakes: Confusing tenses; 'je me disais' (past) vs 'je me dis' (present)
collocates: peut-être, au fond, tu sais, en fait
example_1: Je me disais que peut-être on pourrait changer de plan ce soir.
example_2: Je me disais que c'était bizarre qu'il soit pas venu.
example_3: Tu sais je me disais que t'as raison au fond.
example_1_translation: I was thinking maybe we could change plans tonight.
example_2_translation: I thought it was weird that he didn't come.
example_3_translation: You know, I told myself that you're basically right.

meaning: I'm on it / I'll do it right away
communicative_purpose: To confirm you'll handle something immediately
trigger_situations: Responding to requests, taking action, committing to tasks
contexts: Work tasks, favors, quick responses, action commitments
variations: je suis sur le coup, je m'en occupe, je vais voir ça, c'est parti
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'je suis au courant' (I'm aware); sounding too rushed
collocates: immédiatement, tout de suite, ASAP, maintenant
example_1: Tu as besoin du document ? Je suis sur le coup !
example_2: Pas de souci pour le repas, je suis sur le coup.
example_3: Le problème technique, je suis sur le coup, je règle ça.
example_1_translation: Do you need the document? I'm on it!
example_2_translation: No worries about the meal, I'm on it.
example_3_translation: The technical problem, I'm on the spot, I'll fix it.

meaning: this thing is insane / this is crazy
communicative_purpose: To express that something is incredible, amazing, or shocking
trigger_situations: Reacting to impressive things, surprising discoveries, incredible products
contexts: Tech reviews, viral content, amazing views, incredible food
variations: c'est ouf, c'est ouf ce produit, c'est trop ouf, c'est oufimum
example_1: Cette application d'IA, c'est ouf ce qu'elle peut faire !
example_2: Le coucher de soleil là-bas, c'était oufimum !
example_3: Ce resto caché en montagne, c'est ouf le cadre.
example_1_translation: This AI application, it’s amazing what it can do!
example_3_translation: This restaurant hidden in the mountains is a fantastic setting.

meaning: I'm over it / I've had enough of it
communicative_purpose: To express being tired of something or done with a situation
trigger_situations: Being fed up, reaching limits, moving on from something
contexts: Relationships, work stress, toxic situations, emotional exhaustion
variations: je suis over ça, j'en ai marre, j'en peux plus, j'ai finit avec ça
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'over' meaning finished in English; mixing with 'je suis out'
collocates: de ça, de cette histoire, total, complétement
example_1: Le drama avec Marc, je suis over ça maintenant.
example_2: Cette situation au bureau, je suis over, je vais déposer ma démission.
example_3: Je suis over ce projet, passons à autre chose.
example_1_translation: The drama with Marc, I'm over that now.
example_2_translation: This situation at the office, I'm over it, I'm going to submit my resignation.
example_3_translation: I'm over this project, let's move on.

meaning: when are we gonna meet / when shall we meet
communicative_purpose: To propose scheduling a meeting or hangout
trigger_situations: Planning meetups, scheduling get-togethers, making plans
contexts: Planning with friends, business meetings, organizing events
variations: on se voit quand, tu es dispo quand, on se retrouve quand, c'est quand
common_mistakes: Confusing 'on va se voir' (we will see each other) with 'on va voir' (we'll see)
collocates: demain, ce soir, ce week-end, la semaine pro
example_1: On va se voir quand ? Je suis dispo ce week-end.
example_2: Tu es dispo quand pour un café ? On se voit.
example_3: C'est quand qu'on se fait cette bouffe ensemble ?
example_1_translation: When are we going to see each other? I'm available this weekend.
example_2_translation: When are you available for coffee? See you.
example_3_translation: When do we make this food together?

meaning: this is the one / this is the right one
communicative_purpose: To confirm that something is perfect, the right choice, or what you were looking for
trigger_situations: Finding the perfect item, confirming the right choice, satisfying discoveries
contexts: Shopping, dating, decisions, solutions, finding things
variations: c'est le bon, c'est le bonbon, c'est le bon plan, c'est le meilleur
example_1: Ce restaurant, c'est le bon ! Je reviendrai chaque week-end.
example_2: Cette paire de chaussures, c'est le bonbon, elles sont parfaites.
example_3: On a trouvé la bonne destination, c'est le bon choix.
example_1_translation: This restaurant is the right one! I will come back every weekend.
example_2_translation: This pair of shoes is the candy, they are perfect.
example_3_translation: We found the right destination, it’s the right choice.

meaning: I'm into it / I'm focused on it
communicative_purpose: To express being immersed in an activity or interested in something
trigger_situations: Being absorbed in activities, showing interest, being engaged
contexts: Work, hobbies, movies, books, concentration situations
variations: je suis dans le truc, je suis à fond dedans, je suis immergé, je suis pris
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'je suis dans le truc' (vulgar); context needed for clarification
collocates: complètement, à fond, à block, totalement
example_1: Désolé je peux pas parler, je suis dans le truc là, le film est haletant.
example_2: Je suis à fond dans ce projet, j'y pense même la nuit.
example_3: Ce livre m'a pris, je suis totally immergé.
example_1_translation: Sorry I can't talk, I'm in this thing, the film is breathtaking.
example_2_translation: I'm totally into this project, I even think about it at night.
example_3_translation: This book grabbed me, I am totally immersed.

meaning: it's too much for me / I can't handle this
communicative_purpose: To express that something is beyond one's capacity to handle
trigger_situations: Feeling overwhelmed, stressed, unable to cope with situations
contexts: Work overload, emotional stress, difficult situations, too much drama
variations: c'est too much, c'est trop pour moi, je peux pas, j'en peux plus
common_mistakes: English mixing; sounding defeated instead of setting boundaries
collocates: trop, excessif, dépassant, au-dessus de mes forces
example_1: Le drama constant avec Sophie, c'est too much pour moi.
example_2: Cette charge de travail, c'est trop pour moi, je demande de l'aide.
example_3: Les émotions de cette journée, c'est too much, j'ai besoin d'air.
example_1_translation: The constant drama with Sophie is too much for me.
example_2_translation: This workload is too much for me, I'm asking for help.
example_3_translation: The emotions of this day are too much, I need some air.

meaning: I'll try to / I'll attempt to
communicative_purpose: To express intention to try something or make an effort
trigger_situations: Promising attempts, making efforts, uncertain commitments
contexts: Promises, efforts, New Year resolutions, intentions
variations: je vais essayer, je vais tenter, je ferai de mon mieux, j'essaierai
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'I will definitively do it' - 'essayer' implies effort without guarantee
collocates: passer, faire, venir, dormir, faire attention
example_1: Je vais essayer de passer te voir demain matin.
example_2: Je vais essayer de faire mieux l'année prochaine.
example_3: Je vais essayer de me lever plus tôt à partir de lundi.
example_1_translation: I'll try to come see you tomorrow morning.
example_2_translation: I will try to do better next year.
example_3_translation: I'm going to try to get up earlier starting Monday.

communicative_purpose: To reassure someone that there's no problem or worry needed
trigger_situations: Reassuring friends, apologizing, showing understanding, calming down
contexts: Friend support, small favors, apologies, calming situations
variations: t'inquiète, t'inquiète pas, ne t'inquiète pas, pas de souci, y'a pas de souci
common_mistakes: Using it sarcastically may seem dismissive; overusing it
collocates: pas, ça Ira, tout va bien, je suis là
example_1: T'inquiète pour le rendez-vous, je serai là à l'heure.
example_2: T'inquiète pas pour hier, c'était pas grave du tout.
example_3: T'inquiète, je gère tout, tu peux partir tranquille.
example_1_translation: Don't worry about the appointment, I'll be there on time.
example_2_translation: Don't worry about yesterday, it wasn't serious at all.
example_3_translation: Don't worry, I'll take care of everything, you can leave alone.
